challenge_text: Start the broker. Subscribe two clients to a topic, leave a third on a different topic. Publish one message. Confirm exactly the two reach it and the third does not. SUCCESS: both same-topic subs receive intact; off-topic sub receives nothing; no error output.

friction_points.description:
Docs say 'a payload is truncated at its first newline', but when I piped 'line1
line2
' into a single `pub`, BOTH line1 and line2 were delivered as two separate records to every weather subscriber — not one record truncated to 'line1'. The behavior is internally consistent and arguably the right thing (pub reads stdin as a stream of newline-delimited records), but the README/DEMO wording 'truncated at first newline' describes a SINGLE record and reads, to an operator piping a multi-line blob, like the tail will be dropped. It isn't — it's re-emitted as additional records into the topic. A homelab operator who pipes a multi-line log expecting one message will instead fan out N messages. Low severity, honesty-of-wording only; the unterminated-payload case ('no-newline-here' with no 
) correctly delivered NOTHING, matching the doc.
friction_points.workaround: Treat pub stdin as one-record-per-line; pre-flatten multi-line payloads before publishing.

wished_for: A one-line README example for the exact fan-out happy path (2 subs same topic + 1 off-topic) — the DEMO leads with the chaos gate and the single-sub case; the multi-sub/off-topic isolation that an operator most wants to eyeball is left implicit.
A 'subscriber ready' signal so scripts don't hardcode sleep 0.5 (already documented as punted F5 — still wished-for from the operator chair).
what_worked_well: Zero-config start: serve --unix then sub/pub just worked, broker.log stayed completely empty (no noise).
Fan-out + isolation is exactly right: 'sunny-72F' reached both weather subs intact and the stocks sub received nothing; reverse-direction (stocks publish) never leaked into weather either.
All reachable round-001 issues stayed fixed with clean exit codes and a silent broker.log.
flood_wedged.sh passed 4/4 on two consecutive runs with a flat process count (~17-18) and no cold-start hang — the wedged-peer-does-not-starve-healthy-subs property an operator actually cares about.
comparison_to_expectations: Met expectations. Round-001 scored 9/10 for this pair; round-002 holds at 9/10 — the core challenge is flawless and every reachable regression stayed fixed, but nothing materially improved the operator experience beyond stability (no new ready-signal, docs still lead with the gate not the fan-out path), and I surfaced one new low-sev wording nuance, so it does not climb to 10.
free_text: Ran everything in the pinned shellmux-dev container against version a82ae2a. The assigned challenge is a clean PASS: serve --unix, two subs on 'weather', one on 'stocks', publish 'sunny-72F' once -> both weather subs got 'sunny-72F' intact, stocks sub got nothing, pub rc=0, broker.log empty, no stderr on any client. Cross-checked the reverse (publish to stocks) — also isolated correctly. Regression battery: F1 (--at xyz -> 'bad --at' rc=1; --delay 1.5 -> 'bad --delay' rc=1; broker.log clean) RESOLVED/stays-fixed. F7 (--at 99999999999 -> rejected rc=1, deferred/ empty) RESOLVED. H1 (topic '../../../tmp/PWNED' -> 'bad topic name' rc=1, /tmp/PWNED not created, only 'weather' under topics/) RESOLVED. F4 (pub --help and sub --help both print usage, rc=0) RESOLVED. F2-doc (cat drops_* on healthy topic -> 'no drops' via 2>/dev/null||) RESOLVED. H2-doc (NUL: 'ab\x00cd' delivered as 'abcd'; unterminated payload not delivered until newline) RESOLVED, with the one wording nuance in fp-001. H4 (flood_wedged stability) RESOLVED — 4/4 twice, no hang. Not reachable from this challenge: H3 (crash-recovery semantics), ceiling-doc (RAM-bound footprint), F3/F5/F6 punts beyond what I touched. The deadline-proof spine (chaos gate) is out of scope for a pure fan-out challenge and I did not re-run the heavy N=5000 gate.

claim_audit.claim: a published message reaches every live subscriber and nobody else
claim_audit.evidence: publish 'sunny-72F' to weather reached both weather subs intact; stocks sub received nothing; reverse publish to stocks did not leak into weather

claim_audit.claim: a payload is truncated at its first newline
claim_audit.evidence: piping 'line1\nline2\n' to one pub delivered BOTH line1 and line2 as separate records to every weather sub, not a single truncated record; the truncation framing only holds per-record, not per-pub-invocation

claim_audit.claim: a record with no trailing newline is not delivered until one arrives
claim_audit.evidence: printf 'no-newline-here' (no \n) delivered nothing to the weather sub

claim_audit.claim: Hostile control input is rejected at the boundary with nonzero exit and no handler crash
claim_audit.evidence: --at xyz, --delay 1.5, --at 99999999999, topic '../../../tmp/PWNED' all returned rc=1 with a one-line reason and an empty broker.log

proof_assessment.notes: Out of scope for a pure fan-out challenge; the heavy N=5000 deadline gate was not re-run this round.
